output.context: Post-accessibility changes verification -- checking for visual regressions after ARIA attributes, role/roledescription, aria-live, aria-label, and autoplay toggle button were added.
output.compositionNotes: Overall composition matches the Figma reference at 1440px. The header area with eyebrow, heading, description, and CTA button is correctly laid out with left-aligned heading and right-aligned description/button. The progress bar and navigation arrows sit between the header and card area. Three cards are visible with equal heights, each showing an image at top with rounded corners and a teal/accent background, followed by heading, body text, and a LEARN MORE CTA at the bottom.

observations: Layout matches Figma: 3-column card grid with consistent gap spacing
Header area with eyebrow, heading (Anton font), description, and CTA button is correctly positioned
Navigation arrows (prev/next) and pagination progress bar render correctly in the control bar
Cards display at equal height with accent-colored backgrounds
Card images have proper rounded corners and aspect ratio
Typography hierarchy (eyebrow, heading, body, CTA) is consistent
Dark background section with proper contrast for white text
All three browsers render identically with no cross-browser differences
3-column layout maintained at laptop width
Cards scale proportionally with the viewport
Navigation controls remain properly positioned
No overflow or clipping issues
Cross-browser rendering is consistent
Layout adapts to show 2 visible cards with partial third card peeking
Header stacks appropriately -- heading and description are readable
Navigation arrows and pagination bar remain functional and visible
Card content (image, heading, body, CTA) maintains proper hierarchy
Equal-height cards still functioning correctly
Cross-browser rendering is consistent across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit
Single card visible with proper mobile layout
Header area stacks vertically -- eyebrow, heading, description all readable
CTA button in header displays correctly
Navigation arrows and pagination bar are appropriately sized for mobile
Card images fill the card width with proper aspect ratio
Text is readable and does not overflow
Cross-browser rendering is consistent

details.notes: aria-roledescription='carousel' on swiper-parent, role='group' + aria-roledescription='slide' + aria-label='Slide N of M' on each slide, aria-live on wrapper, aria-hidden on pagination -- all present in DOM but have zero visual impact. No layout shifts, no extra spacing, no visible text from ARIA attributes.

All test page instances have autoplay disabled (data-autoplay='0'), so the autoplay toggle button correctly does not render. The button uses the carousel-nav-btn class (40x48px mobile, 48x48px desktop, rounded-full, border, with Pause/Play SVG icon), which matches the existing nav button design language. When autoplay is enabled, the button renders inline with the pagination bar and nav arrows.
Sections with headings use aria-labelledby pointing to the heading element. Sections without headings use aria-label='Content Carousel'. Both approaches are correct.

noVisualRegressions.detail: Compared implementation screenshots across all 3 browsers at all 4 breakpoints against Figma reference at 785:33970. No visual regressions detected. ARIA attributes are DOM-only and produce no visual output. Layout, spacing, typography, and colors are unchanged.

equalHeightCards.detail: CSS rule '.dynamic-content-carousel .swiper-slide > * { height: 100%; }' is present in swiper.css line 143-144. Visually confirmed in screenshots: cards within each carousel instance align to equal heights across all breakpoints. The card bottom edges (with LEARN MORE CTAs) align horizontally.

imageHoverZoomCSS.detail: CSS rules in swiper.css lines 147-156 are present: '.dynamic-content-carousel .card-post img, .card-team-member img { transition: transform 0.3s ease; }' and ':hover img { transform: scale(1.05); }'. Hover states cannot be verified in screenshots but the CSS is confirmed present.

layoutCorrectAllBreakpoints.detail: 1440px: 3-column layout with full cards visible. 1024px: 3-column maintained. 768px: 2 cards visible with partial peek of third. 375px: Single card visible. All match expected responsive behavior.

crossBrowserConsistency.notes: Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit render identically across all 4 breakpoints. No browser-specific layout differences, font rendering issues, or visual artifacts detected.

tested: Other Initiatives (posts with header description + CTA button)
Meet the Team (team members card type)
Browse Resources (resource card type, no images)
Our Policies (policy card type, no images)
Featured Posts (empty state -- no posts matching filter)
Long heading stress test (extremely long heading + CTA)
